statement: Imagination rules the world.
author: Napoleon Bonaparte

statement: Many of life's failures are people who did not realize how close they were to success when they gave up.
author: Thomas Edison

statement: Of course there is no formula for success except perhaps an unconditional acceptance of life and what it brings.
author: Arthur Rubinstein

statement: Excellence is to do a common thing in an uncommon way.
author: Booker Washington

statement: Be kind whenever possible. It is always possible.
author: Dalai Lama

statement: The true way to render ourselves happy is to love our work and find in it our pleasure.
author: Francoise de Motteville

statement: What we see depends mainly on what we look for.
author: John Lubbock

statement: He who experiences the unity of life sees his own Self in all beings, and all beings in his own Self, and looks on everything with an impartial eye.
author: Buddha

statement: Sadness flies away on the wings of time.
author: Jean de la Fontaine

statement: The only Zen you find on the tops of mountains is the Zen you bring up there.
author: Robert Pirsig

statement: You may be deceived if you trust too much, but you will live in torment if you don't trust enough.
author: Frank Crane

statement: It is not enough to have a good mind; the main thing is to use it well.
author: Rene Descartes

statement: The winds and waves are always on the side of the ablest navigators.
author: Edward Gibbon

statement: Victory belongs to the most persevering.
author: Napoleon Bonaparte

statement: I believe that every person is born with talent.
author: Maya Angelou

statement: It is the quality of our work which will please God, not the quantity.
author: Mahatma Gandhi

statement: If one advances confidently in the direction of his dream, and endeavours to live the life which he had imagines, he will meet with a success unexpected in common hours.
author: Henry David Thoreau

statement: Sometimes the cards we are dealt are not always fair. However you must keep smiling and moving on.
author: Tom Jackson
